Specs: Movement: Rolex Caliber 3085 (automatic GMT, 28,800 vph, 50h reserve); Case: 40 mm stainless steel, thicker case and crown guards, bidirectional 24h bezel with red/black aluminum insert; Dial: Black, tritium lume indices, Mercedes hour hand + red GMT hand, date at 3 with Cyclops; Bracelet: Oyster 78360 steel bracelet; Water Resistance: 100 m

Name: GMT-Master II “Batman” ref. 116710BLNR
Description: A hugely popular modern GMT-Master II variant featuring a black and blue Cerachrom ceramic bezel, earning it the “Batman” nickname. Launched in 2013 (BLNR = Bleu/Noir) on a 40 mm stainless steel case with Oyster bracelet, it was the first Rolex to use a two-tone ceramic bezel. It has a glossy black dial with blue GMT hand. Powered by Caliber 3186, it offers the GMT II’s dual time functionality with independent 12h hand. Production initially ran until 2019 (when replaced by a Jubilee-bracelet version). Its unique colorway and novelty status made it highly sought-after.
Specs: Movement: Rolex Caliber 3186 (automatic GMT, 50h reserve, Parachrom hairspring, COSC); Case: 40 mm Oystersteel, bidirectional Cerachrom bezel split black/blue, Triplock crown; Dial: Black lacquer, white gold lume indices, date at 3, GMT hand with blue arrow tip; Bracelet: Oyster steel bracelet (solid center links) with Easylink; Water Resistance: 100 m

Name: GMT-Master II “Pepsi” 126710BLRO
Description: The modern revival of the classic red-blue “Pepsi” GMT bezel in a steel GMT-Master II, launched 2018. Reference 126710BLRO pairs the bi-color Cerachrom bezel (red/blue) with a 40 mm stainless steel case and, notably, a Jubilee bracelet – echoing vintage GMTs. It uses the latest Caliber 3285 with 70h power reserve. The watch immediately commanded waiting lists, as it brought back the most iconic GMT color scheme on steel after a decade. A keystone of Rolex’s sports line, blending nostalgia with modern tech. Current production, extremely high demand.
Specs: Movement: Rolex Caliber 3285 (automatic GMT, 70h reserve, Chronergy escapement, COSC Superlative); Case: 40 mm Oystersteel, bidirectional red/blue Cerachrom bezel, Triplock crown; Dial: Black, Chromalight lume indices, GMT hand with red arrow, date at 3 (Cyclops lens); Bracelet: Jubilee five-link steel bracelet; Water Resistance: 100 m

Name: Day-Date ref. 6511 “President” (1956)
Description: The inaugural Rolex Day-Date, introduced in 1956. Reference 6511 in 18k gold, 36 mm Oyster case. It was the first wristwatch to display both the date and the day of the week spelled out in full. Usually fitted with the exclusive President bracelet (with semi-circular links). Quickly earned the nickname “President” after being worn by U.S. President Dwight D. Eisenhower and subsequently other world leaders. This model established the Day-Date as Rolex’s flagship dress watch, symbolizing success and status. Early versions had a pie-pan dial and were only made in precious metals.
Specs: Movement: Rolex Caliber 1055 (automatic, day and date mechanisms, 18,000 vph); Case: 36 mm 18k yellow gold, fluted bezel, acrylic crystal; Dial: Champagne with pie-pan edge, gilt baton markers, day of week at 12 (in English or other language) and date at 3; Bracelet: 18k yellow gold President bracelet; Water Resistance: 50 m

Name: Day-Date ref. 1803 (Vintage Pie-Pan)
Description: A classic vintage Day-Date reference produced from 1959–late 1970s. The 1803 in 18k yellow gold (36 mm) featured the hallmark fluted bezel and a pie-pan dial, with day and date displays. It housed the improved Caliber 1556 by the mid-1960s. Worn by numerous presidents and celebrities, the 1803 defined the Day-Date image for decades. Common dial colors included champagne or pie-pan pieced dials; some variants had diamond markers. It represents the enduring mid-century Day-Date aesthetic – elegant, reliable, and prestigious. Vintage pieces remain highly desirable.
Specs: Movement: Rolex Caliber 1556 (automatic, 26j, 19,800 vph, day/date, hacking seconds from 1972); Case: 36 mm 18k yellow gold, fluted bezel, acrylic crystal; Dial: Champagne pie-pan, applied gold indices (or diamonds), day window at 12 (bilingual options) and date at 3; Bracelet: 18k gold President bracelet or leather strap; Water Resistance: 50 m (original rating)

Name: Day-Date 40 (ref. 228238)
Description: The modern Day-Date in 40 mm form, introduced 2015 for the line’s 60th anniversary. Reference 228238 is the 18k yellow gold version with fluted bezel and President bracelet. It features an updated caliber 3255 with 70h power reserve and Superlative Chronometer specs. The Day-Date 40 retains the signature day and date windows but with refined proportions, a slimmer profile, and improved bracelet design (ceramic sleeve links for durability). Often seen with classic sundust or champagne dials. It continues the tradition of the “President” watch for a new era, combining classic appearance with cutting-edge movement performance.
Specs: Movement: Rolex Caliber 3255 (automatic, 70h reserve, Chronergy escapement, COSC + internal Superlative certification); Case: 40 mm 18k yellow gold, fluted bezel, sapphire crystal; Dial: Champagne sunray or other variants, deconstructed Romans or stick markers, day at 12, date at 3; Bracelet: Solid 18k yellow gold President bracelet (ceramic inserts in links); Water Resistance: 100 m

Name: Extra-Plate 5157
Description: A quintessential Breguet dress watch known for its thin profile and timeless design. The Classique 5157 is an ultra-thin automatic (5.45 mm thick) with a 38 mm case in yellow gold. It features a simple two-hand display with no seconds. The dial is classic Breguet: silvered and hand-guilloché with Roman numerals and blued Breguet hands. With its restrained elegance and coin-edge case band, this model exemplifies Breguet’s heritage of refined simplicity and is often seen as an “heirloom” dress watch. Current production.
Specs: Movement: Automatic Breguet Caliber 502.3 (extra-thin, ~45h reserve, offset rotor in 18k gold); Case: 38 mm 18k yellow gold, coin-edge fluted caseband, only ~5.5 mm thick; Dial: Silvered gold, engine-turned guilloché pattern, black Roman numerals, blued Breguet hands; Strap: Brown alligator with pin buckle; Water Resistance: 30 m

Name: Tourbillon Extra-Plat 5367
Description: A flagship Breguet tourbillon in a very slim package. The Classique 5367 features a tourbillon at 5 o’clock on a pristine enamel dial. 41 mm case in platinum, but only 7.45 mm thick – exceptionally thin for a tourbillon. The dial is grand feu white enamel with Breguet numerals and no power reserve or seconds clutter, highlighting the simplicity. The 60-second tourbillon doubles as the seconds display. Introduced 2018, it showcases Breguet’s invention (the tourbillon, patented 1801) in a pure, elegant form with modern technology (silicon escape).
Specs: Movement: Automatic Breguet Caliber 581 (extra-thin tourbillon, 80h reserve, peripheral rotor, silicon escapement); Case: 41 mm platinum, 7.45 mm thick; Dial: Grand feu enamel, printed Breguet numerals, offset tourbillon at 5 o’clock (acting as seconds indicator); Strap: Blue alligator with folding clasp; Water Resistance: 30 m

Name: Classique 7137 Moon & Power Reserve
Description: A Classique model incorporating complications of a moonphase, power reserve, and date in a design inspired by Breguet’s historic pocket watch No.5. 39 mm case in rose gold. The dial is richly guilloché: different engine-turn patterns segment the display (checkered power reserve indicator at 10:30, radial date at 6, and moonphase at 2). Blued Breguet hands and Roman numerals complete the regal look. This watch epitomizes Breguet’s blend of technical function and artistic dial craft. Current collection.
Specs: Movement: Automatic Breguet Caliber 502.3 DR1 (with moonphase and power reserve module, ~45h); Case: 39 mm 18k rose gold, fluted caseband; Dial: Silvered guilloché (hand-engraved on gold) with separate engine-turned textures for each subdial, moonphase and age of moon at 2, power reserve arc at 10:30, date subdial at 6; Strap: Brown alligator; Water Resistance: 30 m

Name: Big Date 5817
Description: A notable model from Breguet’s Marine collection (contemporary sport-elegant watches). The Marine 5817, introduced in the mid-2000s, is a 39 mm stainless steel automatic with a big date display at 6. It blends Breguet’s classic flair (guilloché dial center, Roman numerals) with sporty elements like luminescent hands and 100 m water resistance. It features a solid, anti-magnetic construction and an oscillating weight shaped like a ship’s wheel. Often paired with a rubber strap, it signaled Breguet’s push into modern casual luxury while retaining brand DNA. No longer in production (replaced by later Marine iterations).
Specs: Movement: Automatic Breguet Caliber 517GG (big date, 65h reserve, silicon escapement); Case: 39 mm stainless steel, coined case flanks, screw-down crown; Dial: Silvered with wave guilloché pattern center, black Roman numerals with luminescent dots, big date at 6, blued Breguet hands with lume tips; Strap: Black rubber strap or bracelet; Water Resistance: 100 m

Name: Chronograph 5527
Description: A current-generation Marine collection chronograph, introduced 2018. 42.3 mm case in titanium (for the most sporty variant) making it light and corrosion-resistant. It’s an automatic flyback chronograph with a cleanly laid out dial: 3 subdials (running seconds, 12h and 30min counters) and date between 4–5. The dial is sunburst (blue or slate gray) with applied Roman numerals and lumed Breguet-style hands. The design balances Breguet’s elegance with a modern nautical feel (wave-motif rotor, 100 m water resistance). A sportier, utilitarian side of Breguet for everyday wear. Current production.
Specs: Movement: Automatic Breguet Caliber 582QA (flyback chronograph, 48h reserve); Case: 42.3 mm titanium (or gold variants), ribbed caseband, screw-locked crown and pushers; Dial: Sunburst blue, applied Roman numerals with luminescence, 3 subdials (chronograph hours at 6, minutes at 3, running seconds at 9), date aperture between 4 and 5; Strap: Navy blue rubber strap (titanium deployant buckle); Water Resistance: 100 m

Name: Équation Marchante 5887
Description: Breguet’s grand complication in the Marine line, launched 2017. This 43.9 mm platinum masterpiece combines a tourbillon, perpetual calendar, and an équation marchante (running equation of time) complication. The dial (in slate gray) presents an off-center time display, retrograde date, day/month/year apertures, and a cam-wheeled solar hand for true solar time, all while the 60-second tourbillon rotates at 5 o’clock. Despite complexity, it maintains 100 m water resistance, bridging haute horlogerie and sporty design. The movement’s bridge is engraved with a depiction of a ship. Limited production due to its complexity.
Specs: Movement: Automatic Breguet Caliber 581DPE (tourbillon, perpetual calendar, running equation of time, 80h reserve, peripheral rotor); Case: 43.9 mm platinum (also in rose gold), fluted caseband, display back; Dial: Slate gray, Roman numerals, blued hands; Functions: day and month windows at 10 & 2, retrograde date arc at 9–3, equation of time “sun” hand indicating ±15 min from mean time, tourbillon at 5 o’clock (also seconds); Strap: Black alligator leather; Water Resistance: 100 m

Name: Tradition 7027 (Skeleton Dial)
Description: The original model from Breguet’s Tradition series (launched 2005), which draws inspiration from Breguet’s 18th-century souscription watches. The Tradition 7027 is a 37 mm open-dial watch in rose gold that showcases the movement architecture front and center: main gear train, barrel, and balance are exposed on top of the baseplate. It features an offset black dial at 12 o’clock for time and a power reserve indicator at 10. Manually wound with a 50h reserve. This watch introduced the concept of displaying the “bare” movement on the dial side, influencing many later designs. A seminal piece in modern Breguet history.
Specs: Movement: Manual Breguet Caliber 507DR (open dial architecture, 50h reserve); Case: 37 mm 18k rose gold, coin-edge fluting; Dial: Anthracite black off-center dial at 12 with Roman numerals, exposed movement plates in frosted finish, power reserve indicator at 10, visible balance at 4; Strap: Brown alligator leather; Water Resistance: 30 m

Name: Tradition 7047 Tourbillon Fusée
Description: A highlight of the Tradition line, incorporating a tourbillon with a fusée-and-chain constant force mechanism – a tribute to Breguet’s historical inventions. 41 mm case in platinum. The dial-side features a one-minute tourbillon at 1 o’clock and a cone-shaped fusée mechanism linked by a tiny chain delivering constant torque. There is a small off-center time dial at 7. Introduced 2010 (Tradition 7047), this watch combines a spectacular heritage complication with the raw visual style of the Tradition series. It underlines Breguet’s technical artistry and attention to isochronism.
Specs: Movement: Manual Breguet Caliber 569 (tourbillon with fusée-chain transmission, silicon Breguet spring, ~50h reserve); Case: 41 mm platinum, fluted caseband; Dial: Open architecture, black off-center time dial at 7 with Roman numerals, tourbillon at 1 (60s tourbillon acting as seconds), fusée chain wrapping from center to 4 o’clock; Strap: Black alligator; Water Resistance: 30 m

Name: Tradition 7097 Automatic “Seconde Rétrograde”
Description: A self-winding Tradition model (introduced ~2015) featuring retrograde seconds. 40 mm white gold case. The movement layout is displayed dial-side: a central barrel, a subdial at 12 for hours/minutes, and an exposed balance at 4. Between 7–8 o’clock, a retrograde seconds hand sweeps a 0–60 arc every minute and then snaps back to zero. The watch has a silicon lever and Breguet overcoil for modern performance. The rotor is peripheral (clear view of movement). This piece melds classical inspiration (based on an 1790s “subscription” caliber) with contemporary complications.
Specs: Movement: Automatic Breguet Caliber 505SR1 (peripheral rotor, retrograde seconds, 50h reserve); Case: 40 mm 18k white gold; Dial: Open dial with silvered guilloché time subdial at 12, exposed movement with center barrel and visible balance at 4, retrograde seconds hand over a 180° sector between 6 and 9; Strap: Black alligator; Water Resistance: 30 m

Name: ref. 8928 Mini
Description: A modern Reine de Naples model echoing the historic first wristwatch made by Breguet for Napoleon’s sister Caroline Murat in 1810. The ref. 8928 is a petite ladies’ watch with the collection’s signature oval egg-shaped case (approximately 33 × 25 mm) in 18k rose gold. It features a natural white mother-of-pearl dial with an off-center chapter ring at 6 o’clock and small seconds. The bezel is set with diamonds. Fitted with an automatic movement despite its small size. This elegant jeweled watch exemplifies Breguet’s marriage of watchmaking and haute joaillerie for women.
Specs: Movement: Automatic Breguet Caliber 586/1 (small self-winding movement, ~38h reserve); Case: ~33 × 25 mm 18k rose gold oval, bezel and lug set with diamonds; Dial: White mother-of-pearl, black Breguet numerals on off-center dial at 6, blued Breguet hands; Strap: Satin or alligator strap with gold deployant clasp; Water Resistance: 30 m

Name: Reine de Naples 8908 Moonphase
Description: A complicated Reine de Naples model featuring a moonphase and power reserve. The 8908, often in white gold (~ 36.5 × 28.5 mm oval case), displays Breguet’s lavish ladies’ design with a full mother-of-pearl dial and brilliant-cut diamond bezel. The time is indicated on an off-center subdial at 12 with classic Breguet numerals. Below, a moonphase aperture and a small seconds hand share a subdial at 6, and a power reserve indicator arcs between 9 and 11. With an automatic movement tailored for women, it’s a blend of femininity and mechanical sophistication. Part of the regular collection since early 2000s.
Specs: Movement: Automatic Breguet Caliber 537DRL1 (moonphase, power reserve, ~45h); Case: ~36.5 × 28.45 mm 18k white gold, bezel set with ~128 diamonds (~0.77ct); Dial: Natural mother-of-pearl, off-center silvered subdial at top (Roman numerals, blued hands), moonphase display and seconds at bottom, 45h power reserve arc on left; Strap: White alligator leather; Water Resistance: 30 m

Name: “Day/Night” 8998
Description: A whimsical high-complication Reine de Naples known as the Day/Night Indicator. The 40 mm × 32 mm white gold oval case has a bezel set with diamonds. Its dial is a canvas of fine art: the top half features a rotating disk with the sun and moon (in titanium and gold, respectively) against a lapis sky, indicating day/night as it turns once in 24 hours. The lower half has a mother-of-pearl chapter ring for hours and minutes. The balance wheel is exposed at 7 o’clock as a “seconds” display. Introduced 2016, it showcases Breguet’s creative display techniques for women. Limited production.
Specs: Movement: Automatic Breguet Caliber RJN (24h day/night rotating disk, moonphase, peripheral rotor); Case: ~40 × 32 mm 18k white gold, bezel and dial flange set with diamonds (~143 stones); Dial: Upper dial – aventurine glass sky with hand-engraved titanium moon and gold sun on rotating disc (indicator of day vs night), lower dial – mother-of-pearl hours/minutes subdial at 6 with Breguet numerals, exposed escapement at 7; Strap: Blue alligator; Water Resistance: 30 m

Name: (1953 Original)
Description: The watch that defined the modern dive watch, predating the Submariner. The original Blancpain Fifty Fathoms was developed in 1953 for the French Navy’s combat divers. 41 mm steel case (large for the time) with a pioneering unidirectional rotating timing bezel and double-sealed crown; water resistant to 50 fathoms (~91 m) – a record then. Black dial with bold luminous markers for legibility underwater. Automatic movement with anti-magnetic protection. Famously worn by early divers like Cousteau’s team. This legendary model established key dive watch standards and remains a coveted vintage piece
Specs: Movement: Automatic Blancpain Caliber AS 1361 (with moisture indicator in early versions, 18k rotor, no-date); Case: ~41 mm stainless steel, acrylic crystal, screw-down caseback, bi-directional coin-edge bezel with Bakelite insert (minutes scale); Dial: Black, radium-painted geometric markers and hands (triangle at 12), no date; Strap: Tropic rubber or nylon strap; Water Resistance: ~91 m (first rated 50 fathoms)

Name: Automatique 5015
Description: The modern Fifty Fathoms re-introduced in 2007, channeling the classic’s spirit with contemporary specs. 45 mm stainless steel case, black sapphire-covered unidirectional bezel, and 300 m water resistance. Black dial with large luminous Arabic numerals at 12-3-6-9. Houses Blancpain’s in-house Caliber 1315, a robust 5-day automatic movement. Comes on a sailcloth canvas strap. This watch revived Blancpain’s dive watch lineage and set the template for current Fifty Fathoms models, blending vintage aesthetics with modern size and technology. It remains the flagship diver in Blancpain’s lineup.
Specs: Movement: Automatic Blancpain Caliber 1315 (3 barrels, 120h reserve, no date or with date variants); Case: 45 mm stainless steel, sapphire-capped unidirectional bezel, screw-down crown; Dial: Black, Super-LumiNova Arabic numerals and indices, date at 4:30 (on some versions); Strap: Black sailcloth canvas with tang buckle; Water Resistance: 300 m
